'''Andrew Hay''' (born 5 November 1973) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s, and has [[Coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[Emerging England]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]], and at club level for the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Widnes Vikings]] and the [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster Lakers]]<ref name="Statistics at robterrace.com">{{cite web|url=http://robterrace.com/dons/player.php?PID=907|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180224232639/http://robterrace.com/dons/player.php?PID=907|url-status=dead|archive-date=24 February 2018|title=Statistics at robterrace.com|publisher=robterrace.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and has coached at club level for the [[Castleford Tigers]] (Assistant Coach under [[Terry Matterson]] (2005–11)), [[Hull F.C.]] (Assistant Coach under [[Peter Gentle]] (2012–13)), [[Salford Red Devils]] (assistant coach) and [[Featherstone Rovers]].

==Background==
Andy Hay was born in [[Airedale, Castleford|Airedale]], [[Castleford, West Yorkshire|Castleford]], [[West Riding of Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
Andy Hay appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|ce}} [[Grant Anderson (rugby league, born 1969)|Grant Anderson]] on 63-minutes) in [[Castleford Tigers]]' 33–2 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1993–94 League Cup (rugby league)|1993–94 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 22 January 1994.<ref name="Wigan 2 - 33 Castleford">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/viewreport.php?page=report&id=775|title=Wigan 2 – 33 Castleford|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727175348/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/viewreport.php?page=report&id=775|archive-date=27 July 2011|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Hay played for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] from the substitute bench in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. Hay was an [[England national rugby league team|England]] international and played at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]], scoring two tries in consecutive games against Russia and Fiji respectively.

==Coaching career==

On 16 May 2014, he left the assistant coach role at the [[Salford Red Devils]] to take up the head coach role at [[Championship (rugby league)|Kingstone Press Championship]] club [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/article/30220/hay-leaves-salford-for-rovers|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517121427/http://superleague.co.uk/article/30220/hay-leaves-salford-for-rovers|url-status=dead|archive-date=17 May 2014|title=Hay leaves Salford for Rovers post|publisher=Superleague.co.uk|date=16 May 2014|accessdate=4 October 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9313425/super-league-andy-hay-leaves-salford-to-take-over-at-featherstone-rovers|title=Super League: Andy Hay leaves Salford to take over at Featherstone Rovers | Rugby League News|publisher=Sky Sports|date=16 May 2014|accessdate=4 October 2015}}</ref>

On 25 Aug 2021 it was announced by [[Rugby League Deutschland]] that Andy had been appointed as their new performance director<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Aug 2021|accessdate=25 Aug 2021|title=Former Great Britain international Andy Hay links up with Germany|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-great-britain-international-andy-hay-links-up-with-germany/}}</ref>

On 18 October 2025 he coached {{rlnt|Germany}} in their 72-10 win over {{rlnt|Czech Republic}} in [[Hradec Králové]].<ref>{{cite web|website=European Rugby League|date=16 Oct 2025|accessdate=21 Dec 2025|title=Czechia and Germany Name Squads For Euro D Clash|url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/articles/2572/czechia-and-germany-name-squads-for-euro...}}</ref>. A week later he coached {{rlnt|Germany}} to a 32-18 win over {{rlnt|Norway}}. He shares the head coach role with Bob Doughton.
